subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: g(x) = 2x + ∫₀ˣ f(t)dt from Quarter Circle/Line Graph — g'(x), Abs Max, Inflection & MVT Non-Applicability
stimulus: The continuous function $f$ is defined on the interval $-4 \le x \le 3$. The graph of $f$ consists of two quarter circles and one line segment. Let $g(x) = 2x + \int_0^x f(t)\, dt$.

rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct $g(-3)$: $g(-3) = 2(-3) + \int_0^{-3} f(t)\, dt = -6 - \frac{9\pi}{4}$ (quarter circle of radius 3 below $x$-axis from $-3$ to $0$, area $= \frac{9\pi}{4}$, signed negative since $f < 0$ there)

parts.prompt: Determine the $x$-coordinate of the point at which $g$ has an absolute maximum on the interval $-4 \le x \le 3$. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Considers $g'(x) = 0$: $f(x) = -2$; finds candidate $x = \frac{5}{2}$ (from line segment portion of graph)
1 pt: Identifies $x = \frac{5}{2}$ as the only interior critical point; $g'(x) > 0$ for $-4 < x < \frac{5}{2}$ and $g'(x) < 0$ for $\frac{5}{2} < x < 3$
1 pt: Correct answer with justification: $g$ has absolute maximum at $x = \frac{5}{2}$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $g'(x) = 0$ when $f(x) = -2$, which occurs at $x = \frac{5}{2}$. $g'$ changes from positive to negative, confirming absolute maximum at $x = \frac{5}{2}$.

parts.prompt: Find all values of $x$ on the interval $-4 < x < 3$ for which the graph of $g$ has a point of inflection. Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct answer with reason: $x = 0$ is the only point of inflection; $g''(x) = f'(x)$ changes sign only at $x = 0$ (where the slope of $f$ changes from negative to zero and back, or from one sign to another at $x = 0$)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $g''(x) = f'(x)$ changes sign only at $x = 0$. Inflection point at $x = 0$.

parts.prompt: Find the average rate of change of $f$ on the interval $-4 \le x \le 3$. There is no point $c$, $-4 < c < 3$, for which $f'(c)$ is equal to that average rate of change. Explain why this statement does not contradict the Mean Value Theorem.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct average rate of change: $\frac{f(3) - f(-4)}{3 - (-4)} = \frac{2 - (-2)}{7}$... from scoring key: $\frac{f(3) - f(-4)}{7} = \frac{2 - 0}{7} = \frac{2}{7}$... (check: $f(-4) = 0$ and $f(3) = 2$)
1 pt: Explanation: the MVT does not apply here because $f$ is not differentiable at $x = -3$ and $x = 0$ (corners in the graph), so the hypotheses of the MVT are not satisfied
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Average rate $= \frac{f(3)-f(-4)}{7} = \frac{2-0}{7} = \frac{2}{7}$... (or from scoring key: $\frac{2-(-2)}{7} = -\frac{2}{7}$)... Scoring key gives $\frac{f(3)-f(-4)}{3-(-4)} = \frac{2-(-2)}{7}$... Using $f(-4) = 0$ from quarter circle at endpoint... Scoring key: average rate $= \frac{2-0}{7} = \frac{2}{7}$. MVT inapplicable: $f$ not differentiable at $x = -3$ and $x = 0$ (graph has corners).
